notes: Without-skill agent passed ALL assertions, matching with-skill. Assertions may not be discriminating enough for this eval.
Eval 2 (Tooltip) without-skill baseline FAILED due to 429 rate limit — not a valid comparison. Excluding this, the effective pass_rate delta is +0.20 (with_skill 0.94 vs without_skill 0.80).

Eval 3 (Badge) shows 100% pass rate for BOTH configurations — assertions are not discriminating. The without-skill agent independently discovered the same enrichments. Consider adding assertions for: icon wrapper enforcement, demo organization style, registry/metadata updates.
Eval 1 (Lozenge) is the most discriminating: computed style extraction and audit template format strongly differentiate skill-guided output.
The with-skill tooltip agent made a nuanced shadow decision (shadow-md not shadow-xl) that is arguably more correct than the assertion. Consider revising the shadow assertion.

Time cost: with-skill runs average ~790s vs ~570s without-skill (excluding failed run). The skill adds ~220s overhead from reading skill docs and following the structured workflow, but produces more consistent, structured output.
Token cost: with-skill uses ~62K more tokens on average (skill document + structured output generation).

Qualitative differences not captured by assertions: with-skill uses VPK <Icon> wrapper (without-skill uses raw Atlaskit icons); with-skill adds demos as named exports in existing file (without-skill creates separate files in subdirectory); with-skill updates registry.ts and details/ui.ts (without-skill only updates component file).
